zh.Sutton, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾塞頓

zh.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton是Siward Sutton的兒子，於1156年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾德內斯。他於1172年與Edith Legrose在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1247年在英國英格蘭越可爾塞頓享年91歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton是Siward Sutton的儿子，于1156年出生在英国英格兰越可爾霍爾德內斯。他于1172年与Edith Legrose在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1247年在英国英格兰越可爾塞頓享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: Sutton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Sutton, named with Old English sūth ‘south, southern’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English surname is also common in Ireland (Wexford, Kildare), where it has been established since the 13th century and Gaelicized as de Sutún.
Jewish (from the former Ottoman Empire, including Syria): unexplained.
English: topographic name from Middle English (bi) suthentune ‘(at the place to the) south of the village’.

variants.name: Setton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avi, Ronit, Yossef.  French Andree, Henri, Jacques, Maryse.
Jewish (from the Ottoman Empire): altered form of   Sutton  .
English (Middlesex): variant of   Seaton  .

nameMeanings.name: Sayer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Sayre
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Sayer  .

variants.name: Sears
meanings: Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair ‘son of Saoghar’, a borrowing in Anglo-Norman Ireland of the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (Old French Seheri), from ancient Germanic Sigeheri, Sighari, composed of the elements sig‘victory’ + hari, heri ‘army’. See Sayer 5.
English: variant of   Sayers  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian) Cyr and French Saint-Cyr (see St. Cyr ), with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames.

variants.name: Sear
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Seán (pronounced ‘shawn’), Irish equivalent of   John .  The name has always been common in Ireland, but since the 1960s has frequently been chosen (usually without the accent) by parents who have no Irish connections. One influence on its popularity has been the actor  Sean Connery  (  b. 1930 ), of  James Bond  fame .

variants.name: Searson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5) + -son.

English: variant of Searston, a habitational name from Syerston (Nottinghamshire). The placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Sighere (genitive Sigheres) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
Irish (Tipperary): variant of   Sears  .

variants.name: Sare
meanings: Mainly U.S.: apparently from the name of the herb (Middle English sauge, via Old French from Latin salvia); compare Sorrel and Bay . In part it may also have been taken from the vocabulary word meaning ‘wise’ (from Latin sapius).

variants.name: Saier
meanings: South German: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of Middle High German saien ‘to sow’. Compare Sayer 8.

variants.name: Say
meanings: Short form of Raymond , now also used as an independent given name, especially in North America. In some instances it may represent a transferred use of the surname Ray, which for the most part originated as a nickname, from Old French rei, roi ‘king’ ( compare Roy and Leroy ).

variants.name: Sayers
meanings: English: variant of Sayer , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This name is also established in Ireland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair; see Sears .

variants.name: Sawyer
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who earned his living by sawing wood, from Middle English sauer(e), sauw(i)er, also sagh(i)er, sag(i)er ‘sawyer’, a derivative of Old English sagu ‘saw’.

Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname, or translation into English of Jewish Seger or some other surname meaning ‘sawyer’, e.g. German Sager and Slovenian Žagar (see Zagar ).

variants.name: Seekings
meanings: English: from the unrecorded Middle English personal name Saykin, a pet form of Middle English and Anglo-Norman French Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5). The final -s is post-medieval and excrescent.

en.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton was born in 1156, in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Siward Sutton. He married Edith Legrose in 1172, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1247, in Sutton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 91.

fr.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton est né en 1156, à Holderness, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Siward Sutton. Il a épousé Edith Legrose en 1172, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1247, à Sutton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton wurde 1156 in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Siward Sutton geboren. Er hat 1172 in Yorkshire, England Edith Legrose geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1247 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Sutton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton è nato il nel 1156 nel Holderness, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Siward Sutton. Lui sposato Edith Legrose nel 1172, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1247, nel Sutton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 91 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sayer Suttonは1156年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホールデルネスでSiward Suttonの息子として生まれた。1172年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでEdith Legroseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1247年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアスットオンで91歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton(은)는 1156년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 홀더네스에서 Siward Sutton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1172년 영국 요르크실르에서 Edith Legrose(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1247년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 수턴에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton nasceu em 1156, em Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Siward Sutton. Ele casou-se com Edith Legrose em 1172, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1247, em Sutton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 91 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton родился в 1156 г. в местности холдирнесс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Siward Sutton. Он женился на женщине по имени Edith Legrose в 1172 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1247 г. в местности суттон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 91 год.

es.lifeSummary: Sayer Sutton nació en 1156, en Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Siward Sutton. Se casó con Edith Legrose en 1172, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1247, en Sutton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 91 años.
